title: Papaver taylorii
intro: Papaver taylorii, also known as Taylor's poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae), characterized by its striking purple-blue flowers and hairy, glandular leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it a possible remedy for pain relief and muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of Papaver taylorii include alkaloids, such as reticuline and norreticuline, as well as phenolic compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Papaver taylorii is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, and it is also used in the production of various pharmaceutical and cosmetic products.
